summary: High‑energy 1971 TCU show with monster Other One suite & scorching Truckin'
blurb: The first set launches with tight, upbeat numbers like Bertha and Jack Straw, while the second set builds to a soaring Other One suite that flows into Me & My Uncle, Wharf Rat and an incendiary Truckin'. Jerry’s youthful tone and Keith’s fresh organ work shine, and Phil’s bass drives the band forward. The crystal‑clear soundboard capture makes this one of the era’s standout performances.
review: On November 14, 1971 at Texas Christian University the Dead delivered a high‑octane set that epitomizes the early‑’70s transition period. Set 1 opens with the bright, rollicking “Bertha” and rolls through a string of concise rockers—“Beat It On Down The Line,” “Jack Straw,” “Loser,” and “Mexicali Blues”—showcasing Jerry’s full‑bodied young tone and Phil’s machine‑gun bass thumps. By the time the band hits Set 2, they launch into a monster “Truckin’” that rockets straight into an expansive “The Other One” suite, seamlessly weaving in “Me And My Uncle,” a second run of “The Other One,” and a muscular “Wharf Rat” before closing with a joyous “Sugar Magnolia.” Keith Godchaux’s freshly‑minted organ adds intensity and color throughout, while Bill Kreutzmann keeps a solid groove (even if the cymbals sit just a touch low in the mix). The Miller soundboard capture is crystal clear, delivering separation that lets every instrument breathe—making this performance regularly cited as one of the band’s top‑five shows from 1971. Whether you’re chasing the ferocious “Other One” passages or the bright, tight first‑set swing, this show stands out as a quintessential snapshot of the Dead in full flight.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings label the show as positive and one is mixed, with consistent praise for the Other One suite, Truckin', Jerry’s tone, Phil’s bass, and Keith’s organ work. No negative comments about performance quality appear, so a strong positive adjustment of +1.6 is applied.

best_recording.reason: Crystal‑clear soundboard mix that captures the band's energy, separation of instruments, and highlights the standout Other One suite and Truckin' jams.

key_highlights: Monster "The Other One" suite spanning two halves with seamless segues into Me & My Uncle and Wharf Rat
"Truckin'" erupts with high‑octane improvisation in the second set
Keith Godchaux’s fresh, vibrant organ adds intensity right after joining the band
Phil Lesh’s powerful bass thumps drive songs like "One More Saturday Night"

band_performance.Jerry: Shows a young, full‑bodied tone with clean guitar work and aggressive fills; his lead on "Mexicali Blues" is described as cosmic and he drives the Other One suite with confidence.
band_performance.Phil: Delivers powerful bass thumps and machine‑gun intensity, especially on "One More Saturday Night"; vocal harmonies add confidence throughout the set.
band_performance.Bob: Provides solid rhythm guitar work, anchoring the band’s groove without any noted missteps.
band_performance.Keith: Hits the ground running with vibrant organ/piano that lifts the band, adding intensity and tasteful accents despite still integrating into the group.
band_performance.Billy: Solid drumming foundation; cymbal level is a tad low in the mix but otherwise no criticism.
